---
name: suitescript-map-reduce
description: Use this skill to design and develop Map/Reduce Scripts in NetSuite using SuiteScript, following best practices and guidelines for coding conventions, architecture, and performance optimization.
---

# SuiteScript Map/Reduce Script Development

This skill provides best practices and guidelines for developing Map/Reduce Scripts in NetSuite using SuiteScript. It covers coding conventions, architecture guidelines, and performance optimization techniques to help developers create efficient and maintainable Map/Reduce Scripts.

## Naming Conventions

- Use only SuiteScript 2.1 API for Map/Reduce Scripts.
- Use the prefix `bex_mr_` for Map/Reduce Script files (e.g., `bex_mr_salesorder.js`).
- Use camelCase for function and variable names (e.g., `getInputData`, `map`, `reduce`, `summarize`).
- Use descriptive names that indicate the purpose of the function or variable (e.g., `processCustomerOrders`).

## Script Design and Architecture
- Organize the script into sections: entry points (getInputData, map, reduce, summarize), helper functions, and utility functions.
- Use comments to explain the purpose of each section and important logic.
- Keep entry point functions concise and delegate complex logic to helper functions.

### Entry Point Definitions and Context
- `getInputData`: Entry point for retrieving the input data to be processed. This function should return an array, object, or a search that provides the data to be processed in the map stage.
- `map`: Entry point for processing each key/value pair from the input data. This function is executed for each key/value pair and can emit intermediate key/value pairs for the reduce stage.
- `reduce`: Entry point for processing the intermediate key/value pairs emitted by the map stage. This function is executed for each unique key and receives an array of values associated with that key.
- `summarize`: Entry point for summarizing the results after all map and reduce processing is complete. This function receives a summary object containing information about the map and reduce stages, as well as any errors that occurred.

### Example Map/Reduce Script Structure

```javascript
/**
 * @NAmdConfig              /SuiteScripts/Shared_Modules/config/dc.json
 * @NApiVersion             2.x
 * @NModuleScope            SameAccount
 * @NScriptType             MapReduceScript
 */
define(['N/record'], function(record) {

    // Helper functions for complex logic can be defined here

    return {
        /**
         * @param {Object} context
         */
        getInputData: function () {
            // Entry point for getInputData
            // Delegate complex logic to helper functions
        },

        /**
         * @param {Object} context
         * @param {string} context.key - The key being processed
         * @param {any} context.value - The value associated with the key
         */
        map: function (context) {
            let { key, value } = context;
            // Entry point for map
            // Delegate complex logic to helper functions
        },

        /**
         * @param {Object} context
         * @param {string} context.key - The key being processed
         * @param {Array} context.values - An array of values associated with the key
         */
        reduce: function (context) {
            let { key, values } = context;
            // Entry point for reduce
            // Delegate complex logic to helper functions
        },

        /**
         * @param {Object} context
         * @param {Object} context.mapSummary - Summary of the map stage
         * @param {Object} context.reduceSummary - Summary of the reduce stage
         * @param {Object} context.output - The output generated by the reduce stage
         * @param {Array} context.errors - An array of errors that occurred during processing
         */
        summarize: function ({ mapSummary, reduceSummary, output, errors }) {
            // Entry point for summarize
            // Delegate complex logic to helper functions
        }
    };
});
```

### Governance and Performance Optimization
- Always be mindful of the following governance limits for each map/reduce stage:
    - getInputData: 10,000 API usage points
    - map: 1,000 API usage points per execution
    - reduce: 5,000 API usage points per execution
    - summarize: 10,000 API usage points

## Deployment
- Use the following XML structure to deploy the Map/Reduce Script in NetSuite using SDF:

```xml
<mapreducescript scriptid="customscript_mapreduce_example">
    <isinactive>F</isinactive>
    <name>Map/Reduce Example</name>
    <notifyowner>T</notifyowner>
    <scriptfile>[/FolderPath/FileName.js]</scriptfile>
     <scriptcustomfields>
        <scriptcustomfield scriptid="custscript_mr_custom_field">
            <displaytype>NORMAL</displaytype>
            <fieldtype>TEXT</fieldtype>
            <ismandatory>F</ismandatory>
            <label>Map/Reduce Custom Field</label>
            <selectrecordtype>Map/Reduce Custom Field</selectrecordtype>
            <storevalue>T</storevalue>
        </scriptcustomfield>
    </scriptcustomfields>
    <scriptdeployments>
        <scriptdeployment scriptid="customdeploy_mapreduce_example">
            <isdeployed>T</isdeployed>
            <loglevel>DEBUG</loglevel>
            <recordtype>[customrecord_mapreduce_example]</recordtype>
            <status>TESTING</status>
            <title>Map/Reduce Example Deployment</title>
        </scriptdeployment>
    </scriptdeployments>
</mapreducescript>
```

## Map/Reduce Script Example
- Refer to the sample Map/Reduce Script provided in the `./references` directory.
